senses.definition: used to say that having a particular thing that is considered lesser or lower in quality or worth is more preferable than having nothing at all

examples.x: I didn't win the grand prize, but receiving a consolation prize is better than nothing.
The restaurant was out of my favorite dish, so I settled for a different one—it was better than nothing.
Although the salary offer was lower than expected, I accepted it because it was better than nothing in a tough job market.
The repair job wasn't perfect, but it made my car drivable again, which was better than nothing.

senses.definition: (of something valuable and desirable) extremely difficult to get, particularly due to a lot of people desiring it as well

examples.x: Tickets for the concert were like gold dust, selling out within minutes of being released.
During the holiday season, finding a parking spot in the busy shopping district is like gold dust.
In the job market, qualified candidates with specialized skills are like gold dust for employers.
Limited edition collectibles from that popular franchise are like gold dust among avid fans.
In the real estate market, affordable and well-located properties are like gold dust, often quickly snatched up by buyers.

senses.definition: (of something) still successful, prosperous, or popular

examples.x: Despite the challenges faced by the traditional music industry, vinyl records are alive and kicking, experiencing a resurgence in popularity among audiophiles and collectors.
Despite the emergence of new competitors, the company's brand remains alive and well in the market, attracting loyal customers.
The neighborhood bookstore, despite the rise of online retailers, is alive and kicking, offering personalized recommendations and hosting book signings that draw in book lovers from the community.
Traditional craftsmanship is alive and well in this small town, with skilled artisans creating handmade products that are in high demand.

senses.definition: used to refer to something that is almost ready and can be put to use when needed

examples.x: With the ingredients in hand, I can start preparing dinner right away.
The project is nearing its completion, and we have all the necessary resources in hand to wrap it up.
The presentation slides are in hand, and the speaker is ready to deliver the talk.
We have the necessary permits in hand, so construction can begin as scheduled.

senses.definition: a thing or a set of things considered very dear or valuable to one

examples.x: The archaeologists uncovered a treasure trove of ancient artifacts at the excavation site.
The attic was a treasure trove of old photographs, letters, and memorabilia from the past.
The library's special collection is a treasure trove of rare books and manuscripts.
The antique shop turned out to be a treasure trove of unique and valuable items.
The musician's notebook was a treasure trove of unreleased songs and lyrics, providing a glimpse into their creative process.

senses.definition: not as popular or interesting as before

examples.x: The once booming nightclub is now as dead as a dodo, with only a few patrons showing up on weekends.
Printed newspapers are gradually becoming as dead as a dodo in the digital age.
Vinyl records, while still cherished by some, are considered as dead as a dodo in the mainstream music industry.
The old-fashioned gaming console is as dead as a dodo compared to the latest gaming platforms.
Traditional landline telephones are viewed as dead as a dodo in the era of smartphones.

senses.definition: not functional or in general use anymore

examples.x: After years of neglect, the old factory was now dead as mutton, with broken machinery and empty halls.
The company's outdated business model rendered it as dead as mutton in the modern market.
The once-popular gadget is now dead as mutton, overshadowed by newer, more advanced technology.
The decrepit mansion had fallen into disrepair, its grandeur now dead as mutton, a relic of a bygone era.

senses.definition: no longer useful or fashionable

examples.x: The fashion trends from the 90s are now considered out of date and unfashionable.
The software version you are using is out of date and may have compatibility issues.
The textbook used in the course is out of date and does not cover the latest research and developments.
The company's marketing strategy needs to be revised as it is out of date and no longer effective in reaching the target audience.
The news article contains information that is out of date, as the events it refers to have already taken place.

senses.definition: done for a specific purpose or situation

examples.x: The committee was formed ad hoc to address the urgent issue of employee grievances.
The ad hoc meeting was called to discuss the sudden change in project requirements.
The ad hoc team was assembled to handle the emergency response to the natural disaster.
The company implemented an ad hoc solution to resolve the technical glitch until a permanent fix could be implemented.
The ad hoc committee was tasked with reviewing the proposal and providing recommendations.

senses.definition: (of an achievement) completely worth all the effort one puts in or all the trouble one goes through

examples.x: After analyzing the potential return on investment, they concluded that the new business venture was worth the candle.
He decided that attending the conference was not worth the candle considering the high travel expenses and limited networking opportunities.
The team debated whether the additional effort required to implement the new software feature was worth the candle in terms of its impact on user experience.
She questioned whether pursuing a higher degree would be worth the candle, considering the time commitment and financial investment.
The manager decided that the potential benefits of implementing the new workflow system were worth the candle, even though it required significant training and adjustment.

senses.definition: (of a thing) cannot happen or exist again in the future, due to being completely finished in the past

examples.x: The controversial proposal was dead and buried after it was overwhelmingly rejected by the board.
The once popular trend of wearing bell-bottom pants is dead and gone, replaced by new fashion styles.
After losing the election, his political career was considered dead and buried.
The old factory that had been a symbol of the town's industrial past is now dead and gone, replaced by modern developments.
With the rise of digital music streaming, physical CDs are slowly becoming dead and buried as a popular format for music consumption.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone or something that is good enough to meet a certain requirement or expectation

examples.x: The singer's live performance failed to be up to the mark, disappointing the audience who had high expectations.
The team worked tirelessly to ensure that the final product was up to the mark, meeting all the client's specifications.

senses.definition: simple but good enough to fulfill a particular need or purpose

examples.x: The carpenter's rough and ready tools may not be the most advanced, but they get the job done.
The restaurant's décor has a rough and ready charm, featuring mismatched furniture and exposed brick walls.
The team's rough and ready approach to problem-solving often yields creative and efficient solutions.
His rough and ready attitude may come across as abrasive, but he is always reliable and gets things done.
The company's marketing campaign was a rough and ready effort, executed on a tight budget but still generating positive results.

senses.definition: complicated activities, rituals, or words that lack any real purpose

examples.x: The politician's speech was filled with meaningless mumbo jumbo, leaving the audience confused about his actual stance.
The contract was filled with legal mumbo jumbo, making it difficult for the average person to understand its terms and conditions.
The self-help book promised profound insights, but it was filled with pseudoscientific mumbo jumbo instead.
The technical manual was full of jargon and mumbo jumbo, making it challenging for beginners to follow the instructions.
The conspiracy theory was nothing more than a collection of baseless claims and mumbo jumbo, lacking any credible evidence.

senses.definition: the most important or the greatest part of something

examples.x: The breathtaking view from the mountaintop is the crowning glory of the hiking trail, rewarding the hikers' efforts.
The grand finale of the performance is the crowning glory of the entire show, leaving the audience in awe.

word_list.pronunciation: æz jˈuːsfəl æz ɐ tʃˈɑːklət tˈiːpɑːt

senses.definition: completely useless or ineffective for its intended purpose

examples.x: His suggestion to bring a ladder to fix the roof was as useful as a chocolate teapot since the ladder was too short to reach.
The outdated software provided by the company was as useful as a chocolate teapot for completing the project on time.
Offering him advice on repairing cars is as useful as a chocolate teapot since he knows nothing about automobiles.
The broken compass in their survival kit was as useful as a chocolate teapot when they were lost in the wilderness.
The meeting without an agenda or clear objectives was as useful as a chocolate teapot, wasting everyone's time.

senses.definition: (of a thing) outdated or no longer relevant to current trends or technology

examples.x: His choice of music shows that he is behind the times, still listening to artists from decades ago.
The company's refusal to embrace digital marketing has left it behind the times, losing market share to competitors.
The old television set with limited channels and no internet connectivity is behind the times in the era of smart TVs.
His insistence on using a typewriter instead of a computer highlights how he is behind the times in terms of technology.

senses.definition: a joke, story, or topic of discussion that has become repetitive and uninteresting

examples.x: Oh no, not that old chestnut again!
He keeps bringing up that old chestnut about his high school glory days whenever we get together.
The politician's promises to lower taxes are just another old chestnut we've heard in every election.
The movie relied on tired old chestnuts for its humor, lacking any original jokes.
I don't want to hear your old chestnut theory about aliens causing crop circles.

senses.definition: having reached the desired level of knowledge, understanding, or proficiency in a particular subject, skill, or task

examples.x: A new firm not yet up to speed.

senses.definition: used to describe petroleum or oil due to its immense value and economic significance

examples.x: The discovery of vast oil reserves turned the region into a land of black gold, attracting major investments.
Countries heavily reliant on black gold exports face economic challenges when oil prices fluctuate.
The oil baron amassed a fortune from the black gold extracted from his extensive drilling operations.
The geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East often revolve around control and access to black gold reserves.
The oil boom transformed the small town into a prosperous city, with its economy fueled by black gold.
